search_title: Heading of the search hero section on the main page, above the code input box.
main_title: Name of the tool, shown in the application header. Should normally be left untranslated.
subtitle: Short tagline shown under the search heading, describing what the tool does.
placeholder: Placeholder text of the code input box. It is a sample code snippet (a recursive greatest-common-divisor function) illustrating the kind of input the tool expects. Do not translate the code itself; only comments or identifiers may be adapted if that helps users.
btn_search:
Label of the button that submits the code snippet and starts the search.
{{Identical|Search}}
btn_loading: Label replacing {{msg-mediawiki|btn_search}} on the search button while a search is in progress.
recall_score:
Label for the semantic similarity score shown next to each search result. Value is a number between 0 and 1.
{{Identical|Score}}
no_results: Message shown in place of the result list when a search returns no matches.
show_more: Label of the button that expands a truncated code snippet in a search result. See also {{msg-mediawiki|show_less}}.
show_less: Label of the button that collapses an expanded code snippet back to its truncated form. See also {{msg-mediawiki|show_more}}.
type_all:
Label for result type filter: no type restriction, show every kind of code entity.
{{Identical|All}}
type_function: Label for result type filter: functions and methods only.
type_type: Label for result type filter: type declarations (classes, interfaces, structs) only.
type_template: Label for result type filter: template files only.
lang_all: Label for the programming-language filter chip that removes any language restriction. Kept separate from {{msg-mediawiki|type_all}} and {{msg-mediawiki|group_all}} on purpose: languages that inflect for gender or number need a different form here (Italian "Tutti i linguaggi" vs "Tutte le entità").
group_all: Label for repository group filter: search across all indexed repositories.
group_core: Label for repository group filter: the MediaWiki core repository. "MediaWiki" is a product name.
group_things: Label for repository group filter: MediaWiki extensions and skins together.
group_bundled: Label for repository group filter: the extensions and skins bundled in the MediaWiki release tarball. "MW" is an abbreviation of "MediaWiki".
group_libraries: Label for repository group filter: standalone libraries maintained by Wikimedia.
group_deployed: Label for repository group filter: code deployed on Wikimedia Foundation wikis. "WMF" is an abbreviation of "Wikimedia Foundation".
group_operations: Label for repository group filter: infrastructure and site-reliability repositories. "SRE" is an abbreviation of "Site Reliability Engineering".
group_puppet: Label for repository group filter: Puppet configuration-management repositories. "Puppet" is a product name and is normally left untranslated.
group_pywikibot: Label for repository group filter: the Pywikibot framework. Product name, normally left untranslated.
group_devtools: Label for repository group filter: developer tooling and continuous-integration repositories. "CI" is an abbreviation of "Continuous Integration".
group_analytics: Label for repository group filter: analytics and data-engineering repositories.
group_wmcs: Label for repository group filter: Wikimedia Cloud Services repositories. "WMCS" is an abbreviation of "Wikimedia Cloud Services".
group_apps: Label for repository group filter: the Wikipedia mobile app repositories.
group_search: Label for repository group filter: the translatewiki.net repository. The group id is "search" for historical reasons but it contains only that one repository, so the label names the repository instead. "translatewiki.net" is a website name and must not be translated.
indic_ug: Name of the Indic MediaWiki Developers User Group, shown in the footer as the group the tool author belongs to. "UG" is an abbreviation of "User Group". This is an organisation name; translate only if an established local form exists.
created_by: Label in the footer, followed by the name of the tool author. Includes a trailing colon.
licence:
Label in the footer, followed by a link to the licence the tool is released under. Includes a trailing colon.
{{Identical|Licence}}
issues: Label of the footer link to the bug tracker, where users can report problems with the tool.
source:
Label in the footer, followed by links to the source code repository and its Software Heritage archive. A colon is appended by the software.
{{Identical|Source}}
wikidata: Label of the footer link to the Wikidata item describing the tool. "Q-item" refers to the Wikidata identifier format (Q followed by a number).
toolhub: Label of the footer link to the tool's entry on Toolhub. "Toolhub" is a product name and should be left untranslated.
diff_post: Label of the footer link to the blog post about the tool on the Wikimedia Diff blog. "Diff" is the blog's name and should be left untranslated.
docs: Label of the footer link to the machine-readable API documentation of the tool. "API" is a widely recognised technical initialism and the audience of this tool is software developers; leave it untranslated unless your language has an established localised form that developers actually use.
presentation:
Label of the footer link to the slide deck presenting the tool.
{{Identical|Presentation}}
group_label: Heading of the filter group that lets users restrict the search to certain repository groups.
lang_label: Heading of the filter group that lets users restrict the search to certain programming languages.
type_label: Heading of the filter group that lets users restrict the search to a kind of code entity (function, type, template).
matches_found: Heading shown above the result list, next to the number of results returned by the search.
search_label: Label of the code input box where the user pastes the code snippet to search for.
loading_message: Message shown while a search is running and results are not yet available.
documentation_prefix:
Label in the footer, followed by links to the tool's user documentation. Includes a trailing colon.
{{Identical|Documentation}}
sloan_grant: Funding acknowledgement in the footer. Contains two links, to the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation and to the grant record; keep the HTML markup intact. "Alfred P. Sloan Foundation" is an organisation name and "G-2025-25193" a grant number; neither should be translated.
powered_by_qwen: Credit in the footer for the embedding model used by the search engine. Contains a link; keep the HTML markup intact. "Qwen" is a product name and should not be translated.
data_archived_by_swh: Credit in the footer for the archival of the tool's data. Contains a link; keep the HTML markup intact. "Software Heritage" is an organisation name and should not be translated.
footer_section_credits: Heading of the footer column listing the tool's authors and funders.
footer_section_resources:
Heading of the footer column listing external resources about the tool (documentation, Wikidata, Toolhub, blog post).
{{Identical|Resources}}
footer_section_source: Heading of the footer column listing source code and bug tracker links.
footer_section_status: Heading of the footer column listing the licence and release information.
footer_release_date: Label in the footer, followed by the release date of the current version. Includes a trailing colon.
example_queries_label: Label of the dropdown offering ready-made code snippets that the user can search for instead of typing their own.
example_queries_placeholder: Default, non-selectable entry of the {{msg-mediawiki|example_queries_label}} dropdown, shown when no example has been picked. The surrounding dashes are decorative.
